summary: Build a cab booking service website for Nashik to Pune, closely matching the referenced oneway.cab page.
architectural_notes: Add a simple internal/admin route/page that reads bookings from the backend canister and renders a bookings list for manual checking (no email/SMS notifications).
imageRequirements:
## Edit Images
- Remove the tempo traveller vehicle from the fleet illustration (vehicle-set.dim_1200x400.png > vehicle-set-no-tempo.dim_1200x400.png)

existing_features.title: Create a single-page marketing + booking website for the Nashik → Pune taxi route that closely matches the structure and content style of https://oneway.cab/nashik/nashik-to-pune-taxi.html (hero, booking form, pricing/vehicle options, key highlights, route information, FAQs, and footer contact sections) while using original wording and assets.

Implement booking submission from the frontend: validate booking form inputs, submit to the backend canister, and show an on-screen confirmation state containing a generated booking reference ID and a summary of the entered trip details.

Add basic SEO and trust elements typical of the referenced landing page: route-specific page title/meta description, prominent contact CTA (tel link), and a simple trust strip (e.g., “24/7 Support”, “Transparent Pricing”, “Verified Drivers”).

Design direction: apply a cohesive, travel/transport-themed visual style (not blue/purple) using Tailwind (e.g., warm charcoal + saffron accents, bold typographic hierarchy, card-based sections) and keep it consistent across all components.
Add and render custom static images for the hero and vehicle/pricing section; store true static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend.

Add a manual notifications/admin page in the frontend that lets the site owner view incoming cab booking requests as a list (most recent first), including booking reference ID, customer name, customer phone, and booking timestamp, with a visible loading state, empty state, and a manual refresh action.
Remove the Tempo Traveller option from the vehicle/pricing section and from the booking flow vehicle selection so users can only choose Sedan or SUV.

Update the static vehicle set image used in the vehicle/pricing section so it no longer includes a Tempo Traveller, and update the frontend to use the updated asset filename from `frontend/public/assets/generated`.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a Nashik-to-Pune cab booking service website closely matching https://oneway.cab/nashik/nashik-to-pune-taxi.html
Backend: add a Booking model and canister methods to create and store booking requests for Nashik → Pune trips, including timestamps and status (e.g., “Pending”).
Add a manual notifications/admin page to view incoming cab booking requests (list of bookings)
Remove the Tempo Traveller option from the vehicle/pricing section (and related vehicle assets)

Update the backend canister API to support the manual notifications page by providing a query method that returns bookings together with their booking reference IDs (not just booking values), ordered by timestamp (newest first).
